title: Tapered grip and method of installing a tapered grip
abstract: Disclosed herein is a grip comprising an internal bore with an opening having an internal tapered edge. The grip can engage with a tool or a piece of sports equipment. Further disclosed herein is a method of installing a grip onto a tool or piece of sports equipment, including providing a grip having a tubular body; grinding an open first end of the body, wherein the open first end comprises a greatest diameter closest to the exterior of the bore and tapers internally at an angle towards the interior of the bore; providing a shaft or handle; applying an adhesive to a top portion of the handle; sliding the handle into the grip bore until the shaft contacts the a capped second end of the grip; and coupling a surface of the bore to the handle by allowing the adhesive to dry.
claims: 1. A shaft assembly comprising: a shaft comprising an inner diameter and an outer diameter; a grip; an adhesive configured to permanently couple the shaft and the grip; the grip comprising: a tubular body including an internal bore extending from an open first end to a capped second end, and a body outer diameter which tapers at a constant rate from the first open end to the capped second end; the internal bore defining a length measured from the open first end to the capped second end and a diameter; wherein, the open first end comprises an internal tapered edge having a greatest diameter at an exterior of the open first end and tapers at an angle between 0.5 degrees and 5 degrees for a distance of up to 0.5 inches towards an interior of the internal bore and wherein the greatest diameter is spaced from the body outer diameter at the first open end; wherein the diameter of the internal bore varies linearly along the internal tapered edge; wherein the diameter of the internal bore is constant across a remaining portion of the length of the internal bore; wherein the tubular body comprises a single layer comprising a synthetic rubber material; wherein the adhesive extends between the shaft and the grip along a majority of the length of the internal bore; wherein an amount of adhesive decreases from proximate the open first end to proximate the capped second end; and the outer diameter including the adhesive remains constant across a portion of the shaft received by the internal bore.
